text.title: php-laravel Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for php-laravel as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: $request->all() passed directly to Model::create() or a service method without going through FormRequest::validated().
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: $request->all() includes every key the client sends — including is_admin, role_id, or other protected columns. If those keys are in $fillable, the client has elevated its own privileges.

failure-mode.description: Eloquent::find($id) returning null without findOrFail(), with the controller returning a 200 response with null data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Clients receive a 200 with an empty or null body and must guess whether the record exists. findOrFail() triggers a ModelNotFoundException that maps to 404 via the exception handler.

failure-mode.description: $guarded = [] on a model with many columns, relying on FormRequest to prevent mass-assignment.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Future model columns added without a matching FormRequest update are immediately writable by any authenticated client. Defense-in-depth requires both $fillable and FormRequest validation.

failure-mode.description: new ConcreteClass() inside service or controller methods instead of constructor injection.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Directly instantiated dependencies cannot be swapped in tests. Container-managed dependencies support mocking, decoration, and lifetime management (scoped, singleton, transient).

failure-mode.description: RefreshDatabase in every test class when tests are read-heavy, causing migrations to run once per test class and dominating CI time.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: RefreshDatabase recreates the schema between test classes. DatabaseTransactions wraps each test in a rolled-back transaction — much faster for read-only or SELECT-then-assert patterns.

failure-mode.description: APP_DEBUG=true or APP_KEY= or DB_PASSWORD=root shipped in .env files committed to version control.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Debug mode exposes stack traces and environment variables in HTTP responses. Empty APP_KEY breaks encryption. Committed credentials reach every developer's machine and CI logs.
